response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine breadth — high-throughput micro-services experience at Sociomantic, AI-agent and MCP tooling work at re:cinq, long-running open-source distributed platform, and a polyglot track record that spans embedded, mobile, and backend. However, the resume lacks the Python depth the role treats as a must-have, distributed-systems scale claims are dated (Sociomantic work ended ~2006), and there is little evidence of staff-level cross-functional stakeholder leadership, technical strategy ownership, or incident-response / on-call operations. The AI-native environment exposure is present but thin, and the title inflation (Staff at multiple small/consulting shops) makes it hard to assess whether the strategic scope matches what this role demands.
strengths: Hands-on AI-agent and MCP infrastructure work at re:cinq directly aligns with the AI-native engineering environment requirement
Demonstrated high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems experience at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O and micro-services
Long polyglot track record (TypeScript, DLang, Node.js, C/C++, Java) plus open-source platform ownership shows systems breadth and independent ownership mindset
concerns: No Python mentioned anywhere in the resume — the role lists strong Python as a must-have technical skill
The most relevant distributed-systems scale experience (Sociomantic) is approximately 20 years old; recent re:cinq work lacks concrete scale or reliability metrics
No evidence of staff-level responsibilities such as driving technical strategy, cross-functional stakeholder coordination, or formal on-call/incident-response ownership
